records.ety: [OE. schimeren, AS. scimerian; akin to scīmian, scīman, to glitter, D. schemeren, G. schimmern, Dan. skimre, Sw. skimra, AS. scīma a light, brightness, Icel. skīma, Goth. skeima a torch, a lantern, and E. shine. √157. See Shine, v. i.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Shimmered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Shimmering.]

defs: To shine with a tremulous or intermittent light; to shine faintly; to gleam; to glisten; to glimmer.
quotes: The shimmering glimpses of a stream.

defs: A faint, tremulous light; a gleaming; a glimmer.
quotes: TWo silver lamps, fed with perfumed oil, diffused . . . a trembling twilight-seeming shimmer through the quiet apartment.
